approve.title: Authorize USDC
approve.subtitle: Allow the Factory to move your tokens once

whatIs.title: What is Approve?
whatIs.description: On Ethereum, ERC-20 tokens (like USDC) require you to explicitly authorize a contract before it can move them. It is a security standard: the contract can only spend up to the amount you approve.
whatIs.note: This approval does not move any funds. The movement occurs in the next step.

corpFees.desc: Charged even if the fund performs negatively.

s1.title: Introduction
s1.body: This Privacy Policy describes how FondoRetiro DeFi (hereinafter, 'the Protocol') handles information from users who interact with the smart contracts deployed on the public blockchain. By using the Protocol, you acknowledge that you have read, understood, and accepted the terms set forth herein.

s2.title: Information Recorded on Blockchain
s2.b2: Amounts deposited, withdrawn, and transferred (in USDC or other configured ERC-20 tokens).
s2.b3: Address of the personal fund created through PersonalFundFactory.
s2.b4: Fund parameters: declared age, retirement age, monthly deposit, initial capital, selected DeFi protocol, and timelock period.
s2.b5: Selected investment protocol (MockAaveAdapter, MockOndoAdapter, or others registered in ProtocolRegistry).
s2.b6: User preference settings in UserPreferences (strategy, risk tolerance, autocompounding).
s2.b8: Emitted events: FundCreated, Deposited, Withdrawn, UserConfigUpdated, DepositRouted, among others.

s3.title: Information We Do NOT Collect
s3.b1: Name, surname, identity document, or personally identifiable data outside the blockchain.
s3.b2: Physical address, email, phone number, or contact details.
s3.b3: Biometric or financial information outside blockchain operations.
s3.b4: Cookies, device identifiers, or web browsing/session data.

s4.title: Public Nature of Data
s4.body: Every transaction on a public blockchain is, by nature, public, permanent, and immutable. Anyone can consult the transaction history of a wallet address through block explorers. The Protocol cannot delete, modify, or hide information already recorded.

s5.title: Use of Information
s5.b1: Calculate and execute deposits, withdrawals, and yield distribution.
s5.b2: Verify timelock conditions and withdrawal eligibility.
s5.b3: Route deposits to selected DeFi protocols via UserPreferences.
s5.b4: Record the history of contributions to the retirement fund.

s6.title: Third-Party Data
s6.body: The Protocol interacts with third-party DeFi protocols (Aave v3, Ondo USDY) whose own privacy policies apply independently. Review those policies before selecting a protocol.

s7.title: Security
s7.body: The Protocol's security is based on the immutability of smart contracts and access controls through administrator roles and authorized contracts. The user is solely responsible for the security of their private key.

s8.title: User Rights
s8.b1: Freely access all public information about their fund through any block explorer.
s8.b2: Revoke access of authorized contracts through revokeContract in UserPreferences.
s8.b3: Select and modify their investment protocol and strategy, subject to timelock conditions.

s9.title: Changes to this Policy
s9.body: This Policy may be updated to reflect changes in the Protocol or applicable regulations. Changes will be published in the official project repository.

s10.title: Contact
s10.body: For inquiries related to this Policy, contact us through the official project channels or at contact@ethernal.fund.

s1.title: Acceptance of Terms
s1.body: By interacting with the FondoRetiro DeFi smart contracts, including PersonalFundFactory, PersonalFund, Treasury, UserPreferences, and ProtocolRegistry, the user fully and unconditionally accepts these Terms and Conditions of Use.

s2.title: Protocol Description
s2.b1: Creation of personal retirement funds through PersonalFundFactory.
s2.b2: Deposit and withdrawal of USDC or other stable assets configured by the administrator.
s2.b3: Automatic or manual routing of deposits to registered DeFi protocols.
s2.b4: Management of user preferences and investment strategies via UserPreferences.
s2.b6: Application of configurable lock-up periods (timelock) between 1 and 40 years.

s3.title: Eligibility Requirements
s3.b1: Be between 18 and 70 years of declared age.
s3.b2: Declare a minimum retirement age of 60 years.
s3.b3: Provide initial capital within the configured minimum and maximum range.
s3.b4: Commit to a configured minimum monthly deposit.
s3.b5: Select an active and approved investment protocol in the ProtocolRegistry.
s3.b6: Not have a previously active fund (a user may hold only one active fund simultaneously).

s4.title: Nature of Smart Contracts
s4.b1: Smart contracts execute rules autonomously, deterministically, and immutably once deployed.
s4.b2: No human intermediary can reverse, cancel, or modify already confirmed transactions.
s4.b3: Contracts may contain errors or vulnerabilities. Use implies assuming this risk.
s4.b4: Interaction requires payment of gas fees, the cost of which varies and is the sole responsibility of the user.

s5.title: Timelock Period and Withdrawal
s5.b1: Funds will be locked during the timelock period selected when creating the fund (1 to 40 years).
s5.b2: During the timelock, it is not possible to withdraw funds except under emergency conditions provided for in the contract.
s5.b3: Upon expiration of the timelock, the user may withdraw according to the configured distribution rules.
s5.b4: The Protocol does not guarantee any returns. Rates are estimates from the underlying DeFi protocols.

s6.title: Administrator Role
s6.b1: Configuration of system parameters (capital ranges, fees, enabled protocols).
s6.b2: Authorization and revocation of contracts in UserPreferences.
s6.b4: Recovery of tokens sent by mistake (recoverTokens function).

s7.title: Associated Risks
s7.b1: Smart contract risk: errors, exploits, or vulnerabilities in the code.
s7.b2: DeFi protocol risk: third-party protocols may suffer failures or hacks.
s7.b3: Regulatory risk: applicable regulations may change and affect the legality of use.
s7.b4: Custody risk: loss of the private key implies permanent loss of access.
s7.b5: Liquidity risk: during the timelock, funds are not liquid.
s7.b6: Market risk: the value of stable assets may be affected by extreme conditions.
s7.b7: Network risk: congestion, forks, or interruptions may affect transaction execution.

s8.title: Limitation of Liability
s8.body: To the maximum extent permitted by law, the Protocol, its developers, contributors, and administrator shall not be liable for any direct, indirect, incidental, or punitive damages resulting from the use or inability to use the Protocol.

s9.title: Intellectual Property
s9.body: The source code of the smart contracts is available in the official repository under the published license. The user may audit and study the code in accordance with the terms of said license.

s10.title: Modifications to the Protocol
s10.body: The Terms and the Protocol may be updated. New versions will take effect from their publication in the official repository. Continued use implies acceptance of the new terms.

s11.title: Applicable Law
s11.body: These Terms are governed by the law of the jurisdiction where the Protocol has its development headquarters. Any dispute shall be submitted to the competent courts of said jurisdiction.

disclaimer.title: Legal Notice and Disclaimer
disclaimer.warning: IMPORTANT NOTICE: READ THIS DOCUMENT IN ITS ENTIRETY BEFORE INTERACTING WITH THE PROTOCOL.

s1.title: Nature of the Project
s1.body: FondoRetiro DeFi is a decentralized financial infrastructure (DeFi) project based on smart contracts on a public blockchain. It is NOT a regulated financial entity, bank, insurer, or state-guaranteed fund. Current versions include simulated contracts (Mock) for test environments that do NOT generate real returns.

s2.title: Does Not Constitute Financial Advice
s2.body: No content, functionality, projection, or calculation of the Protocol constitutes financial, investment, legal, tax, or any other professional advice. Consult qualified advisors before making any investment decision based on the Protocol.

s3.title: Risks of Total Loss of Funds
s3.b1: Vulnerabilities, bugs, or exploits in smart contracts of this Protocol or the underlying ones.
s3.b2: Reentrancy attacks, oracle manipulation, flash loan attacks, or other DeFi vectors.
s3.b3: Loss or compromise of the private key associated with the user's wallet.
s3.b4: Forks, 51% attacks, or other disruptions to the underlying blockchain network.
s3.b5: Loss of value of USDC or other stablecoins in the event of depeg.
s3.b6: Regulatory changes that prevent access to or use of the Protocol.
s3.b7: Failures or insolvency of integrated third-party DeFi protocols.

s4.title: No Performance Guarantee
s4.body: The Protocol does not guarantee any returns, interest rates, or capital preservation. Returns shown are estimates based on mock contracts and do not represent contractual commitments. In production, returns will depend exclusively on the performance of the selected DeFi protocols.

s5.title: User Responsibility
s5.b1: Custody and security of their private key and wallet.
s5.b2: Verification of contract addresses they interact with.
s5.b3: Understanding of configured parameters and their financial implications.
s5.b4: Compliance with tax obligations under the laws of their jurisdiction.
s5.b5: Verification of the regulatory status of DeFi protocol use in their country.
s5.b6: Correct approval (approve) of USDC before making deposits.

s6.title: No Deposit Insurance
s6.body: Deposited funds are not covered by any deposit insurance system, state guarantee, or equivalent mechanism. In the event of loss of funds, the user shall have no right to claim before any protection body.

s7.title: Use in Restricted Jurisdictions
s7.body: The Protocol may not be available or may be illegal in certain jurisdictions. The user is solely responsible for verifying whether its use is permitted in their country or region of residence.

s9.title: General Disclaimer
s9.body: TO THE MAXIMUM EXTENT PERMITTED BY LAW, THE PROTOCOL, ITS DEVELOPERS, CONTRIBUTORS, AUDITORS, AND ADMINISTRATOR DISCLAIM ALL LIABILITY FOR DAMAGES, LOSSES, OR HARM ARISING FROM THE USE OR INABILITY TO USE THE PROTOCOL. USE IS AT THE USER'S SOLE RISK.

s10.title: Effective Date
s10.body: This Legal Notice is effective from the date indicated in the header and is updated together with the Terms and Conditions of Use.
